title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Legacy FC debuted in the NWSL with a 1-0 loss to Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium, attended by over 30,000 fans.
The match, part of the Boston vs. New York sports rivalry, saw Legacy FC's Bianca St-Georges receive a red card in the 77th minute.
Gotham's Esther González scored the game's only goal, and a record-tying eight yellow cards were issued during the intense match.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed March 13 at MGM Music Hall, celebrating their 30th anniversary with a fan-engaging St. Patrick’s Day kickoff.
The set included older hits and new tracks, with political themes highlighted in songs like 'Citizen I.C.E.'.
The performance featured guest appearances from Heather Fahey and Austin Sparkman, amplifying the band's powerful and energetic show.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at 11, Bennett supports Joslin Diabetes Center for its role in his life management.
He aims to raise awareness and funds for diabetes, inspired by his personal experiences with the condition.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, a Springfield native, is the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nomination.
Carter, the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design, may become the first to win three Oscars.
Her latest nomination is for 'Sinners,' with historic 16 nominations; she uses Boston-based manufacturers for costume production.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a proposed $18.5 million property-tax override fails.
The district may cut music, language courses, and increase class sizes if the override isn't approved.
Brookline voters will decide on the property-tax override at the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops, reducing reliance on puppy mills.
The legislation promotes collaboration between pet stores, shelters, and rescue organizations to facilitate pet adoptions.
The bill seeks to remove pet ownership barriers in public housing and prevent insurance denial based on dog breeds.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces worsening drought despite heavy winter snowfall, with several regions elevated to serious drought levels.
State officials urge water conservation; Level 3 Critical Drought declared in Central and Northeast Massachusetts.
Groundwater replenishment remains insufficient; Massachusetts Water Resources Authority system not currently in drought.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay discusses a trend of women obscuring their boyfriends on social media to prioritize their individuality.
The trend indicates a shift in how women view relationships, questioning traditional, male-centric approaches to public identity.
Chanté's piece sparked widespread debate, leading to artistic responses and discussions on women’s autonomy in relationships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, from Boston, is running the marathon for The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides children with serious medical conditions a medically safe environment to enjoy being kids.
O'Neill participates to honor his fiancée's sister, Reagan Carey, a camp counselor and cancer survivor.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have paused trade discussions for wide receiver A.J. Brown, despite interest from the New England Patriots.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round draft pick for Brown, which the Eagles rejected.
Trade discussions for Brown could resume post-June 1, potentially benefiting both teams with salary cap adjustments.
